task_objective: Land the lunar module between the flags with a gentle, centered, upright touchdown while using minimal fuel.

progress_variables: vy (downward velocity is progress when high, dangerous when low)
safety_variables: vx (lateral drift must be low at touchdown)
x (horizontal centering is critical for a safe landing)
terminal_evidence_variables: vy (must be near zero at contact)
angle (must be near zero at contact)
x (must be near zero at contact)

phase_structure.purpose: encourage the lander to descend toward the landing site while staying reasonably centered and upright
phase_structure.reward_intent: dense reward for downward velocity that tapers as altitude decreases, plus centering pressure that strengthens near the ground

phase_structure.purpose: penalize tilt and angular velocity to keep the lander stable during the descent, and discourage hard impacts
phase_structure.reward_intent: constant small penalty for deviation from upright; conditional penalty for high vertical speed when any leg touches the ground

phase_structure.purpose: deliver a one‑time success bonus when the lander achieves a soft, level, and centered touchdown
phase_structure.reward_intent: large sparse reward triggered only when both legs are in contact, vertical speed is low, angle is near zero, and horizontal error is small

component_blueprint.name: r_progress
component_blueprint.design_intent: reward downward velocity with a gain that shrinks to zero as the lander nears the pad, preventing the agent from maximising descent speed close to the ground
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: tanh(-vy) saturates, and multiplying by clip(y,0,1) makes the reward vanish once altitude reaches zero; the lander cannot gain infinite reward by descending forever

component_blueprint.name: r_centering
component_blueprint.design_intent: encourage horizontal centering; the penalty grows as the lander gets closer to the ground, forcing final precision
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: the penalty is zero at high altitude, so hovering far away cannot generate reward through centering alone

component_blueprint.name: r_stability
component_blueprint.design_intent: continuously penalise tilt and angular velocity, making upright flight a prerequisite for receiving progress and eventual terminal success
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: the penalty is always present, so staying upright while hovering does not produce net positive reward because the progress term becomes zero when not descending

component_blueprint.name: r_action_cost
component_blueprint.design_intent: penalise thruster use proportional to absolute throttle; absolute value prevents signed actions from being rewarded inadvertently
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: using abs() for both main (0‑1) and side (−1 to 1) engines ensures that any non‑zero throttle incurs a cost, and thrust in one direction never yields positive reward

component_blueprint.name: r_soft_touchdown
component_blueprint.design_intent: whenever any leg contacts the ground, penalise vertical and lateral speed to discourage bouncing or sliding; repeated contacts reinforce soft landing behavior
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: the penalty is active only while a leg touches, so the agent cannot accumulate negative reward after coming to rest

component_blueprint.name: r_terminal_success
component_blueprint.design_intent: grant a large one‑time reward when primitive evidence confirms a successful landing; the event fires at most once (on first satisfying all conditions)
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: the reward is sparse and conditional on multiple stringent criteria; no action can trigger it repeatedly within one episode

anti_exploit_principles: Dense rewards vanish or shrink when the lander is not making progress (e.g., r_progress requires descent velocity).
Stability and centering terms alone produce negative or zero net reward; they only support progress, never replace it.
Action cost uses absolute values to avoid sign‑flip exploits.
Terminal success fires only on a specific set of primitive conditions that cannot be faked after episode termination.
